summary.intent: Review, test, and ship a new 'replicate' mode for git-sync providing source-authoritative relay-only replication (overwriting divergent refs without materialization), then validate it end-to-end against large repos like kubernetes and linux kernel.
summary.outcome: Replicate mode shipped with comprehensive test coverage, a go-git upstream bug fix, adaptive bootstrap batching, cross-branch deduplication, sideband progress streaming, and PACK header pre-check. Validated successfully replicating torvalds/linux (~5 GiB, 11.4M objects) with 18 MB sustained relay memory.

repo: Bootstrap batching used probe-based binary search (download and measure real packs per candidate checkpoint) — replaced with commit-count estimation (64 KiB/commit) plus PACK header pre-check; planning dropped from 4+ minutes to ~25 seconds for linux

go-git's receive-pack unconditionally advertises no-thin (serve.go:45 has 'TODO: support thin-pack'), making all go-git-based servers including entire-server incompatible with thin-pack relay; safe to ignore because FetchPack never requests thin-pack capability

Cross-branch bootstrap deduplication: including previously-pushed branch tips as haves reduces second-branch transfer to 0 bytes when branches share history — linux nocache-cleanup transferred 0 bytes after master was pushed

Commit graph fetches for linux store 1.4M commit objects despite tree:0 filter (all commits, not just first-parent chain); memory.NewStorage() peaks at 4.6 GB but drops to 7 MB after forced GC when the store is explicitly freed post chain-extraction
The entiredb credential helper works automatically when --target-username/--target-token flags are omitted; auth.go chain: explicit flags → Entire DB token → git credential helper → anonymous

Stale temp refs (refs/gitsync/bootstrap/heads/*) from previous runs with different --target-max-pack-bytes cause 'temp ref hash does not match any planned checkpoint'; fixed by finding stale hash in commit chain and re-planning remaining work from that position

Replicate bootstrap empty-target detection uses allAbsent scan of desiredRefs only — orphaned managed refs on target are not checked, creating a gap where replicate --prune on an otherwise-empty target with orphaned refs skips the delete path

code.finding: Double-NAK bug: emits two consecutive '0008NAK\n' pktlines when haves are sent but none are reachable from wants. ServerResponse.Decode consumes only the first; the second is read by sideband demuxer as channel byte 'N' (0x4E) → 'unknown channel NAK'. Fix: gate the second NAK on len(haves)==0.
Unconditionally sets capability.NoThin with comment '// TODO: support thin-pack'. All go-git based servers including entire-server inherit this.
Added drainTrailingNAKs helper to absorb the second NAK pktline before sideband demux in both fetchToStoreV1 and fetchPackV1. Can be removed once upstream go-git PR fix/upload-pack-double-nak lands.

planCheckpointsFromChain uses avgBytesPerCommit=65536. Stores full commit chain in memory for O(log n) adaptive subdivision. PACK header pre-check reads 12 bytes (object count) to estimate pack size; aborts source fetch before streaming if over --target-max-pack-bytes.

BuildReplicationPlans calls copyManagedTargets internally to avoid mutating the caller's managed map. Was flagged as a footgun in review; behavior locked in via TestBuildReplicationPlansDoesNotMutateManaged.
CanReplicateRelay previously rejected no-thin targets. Relaxed because FetchPack never requests thin-pack capability, so the relayed pack is always self-contained and safe to push to no-thin targets.
Breaking rename: ExecutionSummary.Mode → TransferMode (engine path). New OperationMode field for sync/replicate. No compatibility alias. Documented in CHANGELOG.
defaultOperationMode had a dead branch: name=="plan" returned ModeSync identical to the fallback. Simplified by removing the name parameter entirely.

Sideband progress from receive-pack was silently discarded (nil sink). Now routed to os.Stderr with 'target: ' prefix when Verbose=true. Source progress enabled by omitting no-progress capability when Verbose=true.

workflow: Testing against real large repos (linux kernel) is essential for bootstrap/batching issues invisible in unit tests — the full test suite passed but planning took 4+ min and memory peaked at 6 GB on linux before fixes
GODEBUG=gctrace=1 is the right tool for diagnosing memory in long-running Go operations — reveals exact heap sizes at each GC pause and shows precisely when stores are freed vs retained
When filing upstream bugs, craft a minimal failing test in the upstream repo first, then add the fix — makes the PR self-contained and reviewable

Estimate + safety-net patterns beat probe-based measurement when the data being measured is the data you'll eventually transfer; the 12-byte PACK header object count gives a cheap size signal before committing to GB-scale transfers
Token expiry is a real operational concern for operations taking 15+ min; minimizing planning time (25s vs 4 min) keeps runs within typical OAuth token lifetimes

friction: Auth confusion: --target-username/--target-token sends HTTP Basic auth which entire-server rejects with 'Invalid token format'; entiredb OAuth tokens only work when those flags are omitted and the credential chain falls through to the keychain
TLS: local entire-server uses HTTPS; initial commands used http:// causing cryptic 'Client sent HTTP request to HTTPS server' before --target-insecure-skip-tls-verify was identified as needed
Token expiry mid-run: OAuth tokens expired during long bootstrap operations requiring re-login; manifested as http 401 only at the push phase after 30+ min of planning and source fetches
Misleading 'replicate-target-no-thin' error appeared before the no-thin design decision was revisited — looked like a capability mismatch but was actually a policy choice that could be relaxed
Subdivide retry loop bug: continue in a for idx++ loop incremented past the inserted midpoint, causing the same oversized batch to retry forever before the index management was fixed

The --batch-max-pack-bytes flag controlled both the planning estimate AND the LimitPackReader source fetch cap, causing the 1 GiB batch size test to abort fetches with 'source pack exceeded max-pack-bytes limit'

Stale temp refs from previous runs with different batch settings caused 'temp ref hash does not match any planned checkpoint' requiring manual git push --delete before the auto-cleanup logic was added
GODEBUG=gctrace=1 output interleaves with structured log lines and JSON output in the tee'd log making it noisy to parse
open_items: drainTrailingNAKs in internal/gitproto/fetch.go should be removed once go-git PR fix/upload-pack-double-nak is merged and the dependency bumped
go-git upstream PR (../../go-git branch fix/upload-pack-double-nak) needs to be submitted to go-git maintainers
Commit graph fetch transient memory spike (~4.6 GB for linux, 1.4M commits) is unavoidable until go-git's pack parser supports streaming decode or a first-parent-only protocol filter exists
Relay phase backpressure: HTTP transport buffers source→target data proportional to the speed differential; application-layer flow control not implemented

Ref prefix transformation (e.g., refs/heads/main → refs/gh/heads/main for multi-source mirroring) discussed but not implemented — needs --target-prefix flag, BuildDesiredRefs changes, RefKind generalization beyond refs/heads/ and refs/tags/, relay eligibility based on source kind

Three review items from initial review still open: dryRun parameter in CanReplicateRelay is hardcoded false at every call site, SupportsReplicateRelay called twice in syncer.go, usage string in cmd/git-sync/main.go is duplicated
No end-to-end test for replicate --prune against a target that has orphaned managed refs but all desiredRefs absent (allAbsent check gap noted in review)
